pairs.question: Who was the head coach of the UCF Knights football team during the 2018 season?
pairs.answer: The head coach of the UCF Knights during the 2018 season was Josh Heupel, who was in his first year as head coach.

pairs.question: What was the final record of the UCF Knights in the 2018 regular season?
pairs.answer: The UCF Knights finished the 2018 regular season undefeated, with a record of 12–0.

pairs.question: How many consecutive games did UCF win before losing in the 2018 Fiesta Bowl?
pairs.answer: UCF won 25 consecutive games before losing to LSU in the 2018 Fiesta Bowl. Their streak began at the start of the 2017 season.

pairs.question: What was the UCF Knights' ranking in the AP Poll by week 12 of the 2018 season?
pairs.answer: By week 12 of the 2018 season, the UCF Knights had risen to #8 in the AP Poll.

pairs.question: Which quarterback led the UCF Knights to victory in the 2018 AAC Championship Game?
pairs.answer: Backup quarterback Darriel Mack Jr. led the UCF Knights to victory in the 2018 AAC Championship Game after starting quarterback McKenzie Milton suffered an injury.

pairs.question: How many AAC conference titles did UCF win in six years leading up to the 2018 season?
pairs.answer: UCF won four AAC conference titles in six years, with titles in 2013, 2014, 2017, and 2018.

pairs.question: What was the UCF Knights' offensive strategy under Josh Heupel, and how did it perform statistically?
pairs.answer: Under Josh Heupel, UCF employed an explosive no-huddle offense. Statistically, they ranked 5th in the nation in total yards per game (522.7) and 6th in points per game (43.2).
